communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please see our attached open records request for our client Willie Morris regarding date of loss 03/24/2026. I have attached a copy of the police report for your reference. If you need additional information in order to process our request, please let me know.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Gerstner

title: Preciado Capital Murder Investigation - Records Request (Soto/Guerra, 2023)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request copies of the following records related to the San Antonio Police Department's investigation and arrest of Christopher Preciado for the December 2023 capital murders of Savanah Soto and Matthew Guerra:

1. All body-worn camera video from SAPD officers who responded to the initial scene, any welfare checks, the recovery of the victims, and/or the arrest of Christopher Preciado
2. All 911 audio recordings and CAD (Computer-Aided Dispatch) records for the incident
3. The full initial incident/offense report and any supplemental reports
4. The arrest report and probable cause affidavit for Christopher Preciado
5. Any recorded custodial interview or interrogation of Christopher Preciado
6. Crime scene photographs, diagrams, and evidence logs
7. Any press releases or public statements issued by SAPD regarding the case

Note: I previously filed a similar request with the Bexar County Sheriff's Office, and their records custodian advised me that this was a San Antonio Police Department investigation and redirected me to SAPD. I am filing this request accordingly.

Please provide records in electronic format where possible to minimize cost. I am willing to pay reasonable duplication fees up to $100 without prior approval; please notify me if fees will exceed this amount before producing records.

This case has been fully adjudicated (guilty verdict on three counts of capital murder, March 26, 2026), so exemptions for active criminal investigations under Texas Government Code Chapter 552 should no longer apply. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days as required by the Texas Public Information Act.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ka Yi Lo
